topic: EXP-407 footnote (i) penumbra two-reading derivation — adversarial review of integrated text (post-EXP-407, post-EXP-409). Math verified; one fresh moderate (duplicate paragraph) introduced by EXP-409 sitting alongside this integration.
change_summary: EXP-407 replaced the 51-word hand-wavy footnote (i) ("weakly non-linear … still exceeds 90× the dome's published 2,534 km, so the kill is unaffected") with a 155-word exact two-reading derivation: (a) wholesale rescaling, R_p ∝ h_Moon exact only if (h_S, h_M, R_S, R_M) all scaled together by 97.24×, requiring h_M = 246,401 km AND h_S = 557,466 km; (b) partial scaling with h_S fixed, exact formula R_p = R_M + (R_S + R_M)·h_M/(h_S − h_M) diverges as h_M → h_S, R_p = 4,084 km requires h_M = 5,660 km (Moon within 73 km of the firmament Sun). Conclusion upgrades from "kill unaffected" to "unaffected under linear charity, strengthened under exact geometry". Closes ISS-1643 (c2/c3 H2). Parallel patch EXP-409 inserted the R_disc/180 cross-convention parenthetical into the constant-r subsection — closes ISS-1747 (c3 H4) but introduced a duplicate-paragraph bug (see hole H1 below).
our_argument_summary: SEC-4.2.7a (Part 4, station-by-station coverage under dome geometry) quantifies the "Borrowed" branch of the §4.2.7 trilemma: dome-published h_Sun=5,733 km / h_Moon=2,534 km plus the August 12 2026 NASA umbra path forces every one of the dome's nine published eclipse stations 444–4,084 km from the projected ground path — far outside the 42-km penumbra radius (85-km band). The footnote (i) post-EXP-407 now closes the linearization pedant attack with an exact two-reading derivation. The body continues to argue: 0/9 stations in band under the standard convention; max 1–2 stations under stricter R_disc/180 (per EXP-409 cross-reference); ≥97× h_Moon inflation required for the dome's prediction to land; 2D Euclidean MST = 11,696 km vs realistic 4,500 km mid-disc 3-hour arc (2.6× ratio); time-distribution rebuttal pre-empted. The kill is now genuinely overdetermined: linear-charity arithmetic gives 97× inflation, exact-geometry arithmetic forces either absurd absolute altitudes (h_S = 557,466 km) or Moon-Sun co-location (within 73 km). Verdict survives this fourth pass.

relevant_files: data/sections.json:part4.html SEC-4.2.7a section (read fresh from clone HEAD b3e8746e)
raw-text/03-predictions.txt (dome's 9-station coverage + FSF table for August 12 2026 eclipse; SNK = Canary Islands, Coverage 0.72, FSF 0.642)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No monitor.py path. EXP-407 is a prose insertion. Math independently reproduced: R_S = R_p / (r + (1+r)·k) with r = h_M/h_S = 0.442, k = h_M/(h_S − h_M) = 0.792 → R_S = 26.5110 km, R_M = R_S · r = 11.7179 km, Sun diameter 53.02 km. Wholesale factor f = 4084/42 = 97.2381, so h_M·f = 246,401.3 km and h_S·f = 557,466.0 km (matches footnote 246,401 / 557,466). Partial scaling bisection (h_S fixed at 5,733): h_M = 5,659.52 km → distance below Sun = 73.48 km (matches footnote 5,660 / within 73 km). EXP rationale table values (R_p at h_M = 2534, 3000, 4000, 5000, 5500, 5600, 5700, 5720, 5730 → 42, 58, 122, 362, 1252, 2233, 9158, 23330, 101272 km) all reproduce within ±0.5 km.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — section is prose. The 42-km baseline penumbra and 4,084-km Canary Islands distance are dome-section-internal computed quantities, both reproduced independently.

INTERMAGNET observatory coordinates — per-station distance-to-path baseline (unchanged from c3)
IAGA registry (28.321° N, 16.432° W for GUI/Güimar, Tenerife)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: EXP-407 explicitly closes the prior escape clause ("weakly non-linear ... still exceeds 90× ... kill unaffected") with formula-cited exact geometry. The new footnote framing eliminates the hand-wave; no fresh escape clause introduced.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None at the math level. The simplification R_p = 2 R_S h_M / (h_S − h_M) under the angular-size constraint R_M = R_S · h_M/h_S — which is the algebraic kernel revealing why h_M → h_S forces divergence — is not stated explicitly in the footnote (it would tighten the geometric story without adding length); see hole H4 below. Code-tag findings unchanged (section-level review; no per-WIN tags).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Section-level review; section integrates the dome's tabular eclipse predictions but does not represent a per-WIN monitor.py path.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section-level review; the kill argument uses standard penumbra geometry applied to dome-published altitudes. No relabeling claim.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Section-level review; the dome's eclipse predictions are dated 2026-03-12 and the August 12, 2026 event is prospective.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Section-level review; the kill is derived from dome-published h_Sun = 5,733 km, h_Moon = 2,534 km, R_disc = 20,015 km, and the angular-size constraint the dome explicitly endorses elsewhere (W021, lunar angular-diameter content).

holes_found.description: Duplicate paragraph in the constant-r subsection. The R_disc/180 cross-reference parenthetical ("(Under the stricter R_disc/180 projection convention treated in the P1 robustness paragraph above, this pair's Δr halves to ≈ 75 km, and Ebro–Coimbra to ≈ 82 km — both inside the 85-km band — which softens the constant-r reading to "max 2" rather than max 1; the 0/9 headline survives either convention.)") appears TWICE back-to-back in data/sections.json:part4.html — once with HTML entities (“…” and right-single-quote ’) at offset ~92011, once with literal ASCII quotes at offset ~92348. Git blame: the first occurrence was added by a decider/baby run between commits 5e798cb5 (2026-05-14, 0 occurrences) and 896652f1 (2026-05-17T01:23Z, 1 occurrence); the duplicate was added by commit 9c86f78b (operator-direct integration 2026-05-17T07:26Z, applying EXP-409 "SEC-4.2.7a cross-convention xref +343 chars closes ISS-1747") on top of the already-present first copy. A reader sees the same sentence twice in a row — visible credibility damage, especially in a section that has just been carefully tightened.
holes_found.recommendation: Delete one occurrence (preferably the literal-quote second copy at ~92348, retaining the HTML-entity version that matches surrounding prose style). Re-open ISS-1747 (currently status:fixed in closed-issues) OR file a fresh issue for the EXP-409 integration regression. Defense-in-depth: add a tests/run.sh check for substring duplication in sections.json — a regex of the form /)s+(Under the stricter R<sub>disc</sub>/180/ should match zero times.
holes_found.affects: constant-r subsection in part4.html (one duplicate parenthetical, ~270 chars to delete).

holes_found.description: Footnote (i) presents the wholesale and partial-h_S-fixed cases as if they were the only two readings, but the constraint R_p = 4,084 km with R_S held at the calibrated 26.5 km admits a continuous family of (h_S, h_M) solutions. Under the angular-size match R_M = R_S · h_M/h_S, the penumbra simplifies algebraically to R_p = 2 R_S h_M / (h_S − h_M). Setting that to 4,084 with R_S = 26.5 gives h_M/(h_S − h_M) ≈ 77, so h_M ≈ 0.987 h_S — the Moon-Sun altitude co-location is GENERIC across the whole family, not specific to the h_S = 5,733 corner. The current text says "the dome's published parameter set cannot produce a 4,084-km penumbra under either reading," which is correct but invites the pedant question "what about an intermediate h_S?" — and the actual answer is stronger than the current text reveals (any intermediate scaling preserving R_S near its calibrated value forces the same Moon-Sun co-location).
holes_found.recommendation: One additional clause after the partial-scaling paragraph: "Intermediate scalings (h_S held at some value between 5,733 and 557,466 km) lie on the same h_M ≈ 0.987 h_S curve under R_S held near its 26.5-km calibration — the Moon-Sun altitude co-location is generic, not specific to the h_S = 5,733 corner." Adds ~30 words; tightens the closure without doubling the footnote length.

holes_found.description: The phrase "putting the Moon within 73 km of the firmament Sun itself" is parseable two ways: (a) "Sun-which-is-attached-to-the-firmament structure" (the intended reading, defended in EXP-407 anticipated-objection #3), or (b) "Sun at the firmament altitude of 8,537 km". A reader who has been tracking the dome's parameter set (h_firmament = 8,537 km, h_Sun = 5,733 km) and hits "firmament Sun" without disambiguation may stall and re-read. The EXP rationale acknowledges this and chose to defend the wording; the reader doesn't see the EXP rationale.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace "putting the Moon within 73 km of the firmament Sun itself" with "putting the Moon's altitude within 73 km of the dome's published 5,733-km Sun altitude" — disambiguates without losing rhetorical punch. Two-word edit.

holes_found.description: The calibration R_S ≈ 26.5 km / R_M ≈ 11.7 km is derived in the EXP-407 rationale but invisible in the footnote text. A reader of the footnote alone sees the formula R_p = R_M + (R_S + R_M)·h_M/(h_S − h_M) but cannot audit the derivation of either endpoint number (246,401 km or 5,660 km) without the body context — the relation between R_p = 42 km baseline (established in the section body) and (R_S, R_M) values is implicit. A pedant who skipped to the footnote might ask: "where do these specific h_M values come from?"
holes_found.recommendation: Optional one-clause insertion right after the formula: "(R_S ≈ 26.5 km and R_M ≈ 11.7 km follow from calibrating the formula to the dome's baseline R_p ≈ 42 km under angular-size match R_M/h_M = R_S/h_S.)" — makes the derivation auditable from the footnote alone. ~25 words. Could be deferred as polish.
holes_found.affects: footnote (i), one optional bracketed clause (~25 words).

stronger_arguments.description: Symmetric simplification under angular-size match: R_p = 2 R_S h_M / (h_S − h_M). This single-line algebraic form makes the entire kill transparent: R_p scales linearly in R_S and as h_M/(h_S − h_M) — the divergence at h_M → h_S is structural, not accidental. A defender who wants to inflate R_p has three knobs (R_S up, h_M closer to h_S, both proportionally) and ALL three force absurd-by-dome-standards parameter sets. Adding this formula as a one-line corollary in the footnote would let a numerate reader verify both extreme cases (and any intermediate) from a single equation — strictly stronger than the current presentation, which derives the two cases from the full formula independently. Sources: derivation in this review's deeper_analysis section; standard finite-source penumbra geometry (any optics/astronomy textbook treating the umbra-penumbra cone).
sources: standard optics: penumbra cone for a finite light source occluded by a finite spherical body

stronger_arguments.description: EXP-407 + EXP-409 + the prior MST patch jointly make SEC-4.2.7a a near-canonical example of "two complementary attacks on the same kill": exact-geometry (this footnote) and projection-convention robustness (EXP-409 cross-reference). Folding a single explicit sentence into the section opening — "This section establishes the kill under both linear and exact geometry, across two projection conventions, and under the strict constant-r reading" — would let a curmudgeon-fatigued reader (or a defender skimming for entry points) know up front that all four robustness axes have been engaged. Carryover from c3 stronger_argument #1 (FSF/latitude correlation) still un-folded; tracker says "out of scope for the robustness-fix track."
sources: EXP-276 + EXP-407 + EXP-409 integrated text in part4.html

kernel_of_truth.description: EXP-407 is a textbook example of the right way to close a pedant attack: identify the conflation in the original hand-wave, derive both readings explicitly, name the required parameter set in each, and let the conclusion follow from the geometry rather than from rhetorical re-assertion. The 51-word footnote that previously had a "corrected ratio still exceeds 90×" assertion with no derivation now has a 155-word footnote with the exact formula, two named cases, and four specific numerical answers (97.24×, 246,401 km, 557,466 km, 5,660 km, 73 km). The dome-source kernel-of-truth — that R_p ∝ h_Moon IS exact under wholesale rescaling — is acknowledged directly and turned against the dome (the wholesale rescaling requires h_S = 557,466 km, contradicting every other dome parameter). The independent reproduction of all four numbers, the divergence table, and the calibrated (R_S, R_M) all hold to ±0.5 km on bisection. The footnote is mathematically watertight as written; my four holes are about presentation completeness, not arithmetic. (data/sections.json:part4.html footnote-i; EXP-407 monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-407-SEC-4.2.7a-penumbra-scaling-correction.json rationale_summary fields.)
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: N/A — this review is on the offensive side and the kernel is in the EXP, not the dome. The structural kill survives: the dome's nine published eclipse stations are 444–4,084 km from the path under any consistent dome parameter set; reaching them requires either a Moon at ten times the firmament altitude or a Moon nearly co-located with the Sun. Neither is a survivable dome configuration. (data/sections.json:part4.html headline paragraph; raw-text/03-predictions.txt E001–E009 station list.)

integration_markers_verified: kill is unaffected under linear charity
integration_anti_markers_present_but_sho: duplicate "softens the constant-r reading" paragraph (count = 2; expected = 1) — see hole H1

deeper_analysis: Math reproduction (independent of EXP-407 rationale): under R_p = R_M + (R_S + R_M)·h_M/(h_S − h_M) with angular-size constraint R_M = R_S · h_M/h_S, substituting and simplifying gives R_p = 2 R_S h_M / (h_S − h_M). With dome parameters (h_S = 5,733 km, h_M = 2,534 km) and R_p = 42 km baseline, the unique R_S is 26.5110 km (Sun diameter ≈ 53 km, consistent with the classical flat-earth ~32-mile Sun figure). Wholesale rescaling by f = 4,084/42 = 97.2381 gives h_M = 246,401.3 km and h_S = 557,466.0 km exactly (footnote rounds to 246,401 and 557,466). Partial scaling with h_S = 5,733 fixed and R_M tracking angular-size: solving R_p_partial(h_M) = 4,084 by bisection on (2,534, 5,732.99) converges to h_M = 5,659.52 km with distance-below-Sun = 73.48 km (footnote rounds to 5,660 and 73). Divergence table at h_M ∈ {2534, 3000, 4000, 5000, 5500, 5600, 5700, 5720, 5730} → R_p ∈ {42, 58, 122, 362, 1252, 2233, 9158, 23330, 101272} km reproduces the EXP-407 rationale's table within ±0.5 km. The kill is mathematically watertight. Generic argument under angular-size match: from R_p = 2 R_S h_M / (h_S − h_M) with R_p = 4,084 and R_S held at 26.5, h_M/(h_S − h_M) ≈ 77 forces h_M ≈ 0.987 h_S — so EVERY (h_S, h_M) on the kill surface with R_S near its calibrated value has the Moon at ~98.7% of the Sun's altitude. The Moon-Sun co-location is structural, not contingent. This is the strongest single-line summary of the kill. The integration carries one fresh moderate-severity bug introduced by EXP-409 (duplicate paragraph in the constant-r subsection — see hole H1) plus three minor presentation tightenings on the EXP-407 footnote itself. ISS-1643 (the c2/c3 hand-wave hole) is closed by EXP-407; ISS-1747 (the c3 constant-r convention dependency) is closed by EXP-409 but with the duplicate-paragraph regression. Verdict-bearing structural argument unchanged; the kill is now genuinely overdetermined.

pre_write_staleness_check.findings_re_checked: All four holes verified present in current content. H1 (duplicate paragraph): two occurrences at offsets ~92011 and ~92348 in part4.html. H2 (intermediate scaling): footnote (i) reads exactly as in the EXP-407 patch with the two-case framing. H3 ("firmament Sun" phrasing): present in current footnote. H4 (calibration not shown inline): footnote (i) does not include the (R_S, R_M) calibration clause.

summary_for_decider: queue_id 368 (SEC-4.2.7a EXP-407) — recommended_action: minor_edit. EXP-407 itself is well-executed and closes ISS-1643 cleanly; my math reproduces all four endpoint numbers (97.24×, 246,401 km, 557,466 km, 5,660 km, 73 km) and the EXP rationale table to ±0.5 km. ONE FRESH MODERATE: EXP-409 integration regression — the R_disc/180 cross-convention parenthetical (closing ISS-1747) appears DUPLICATED back-to-back in the constant-r subsection (~offsets 92011 and 92348 in part4.html, one HTML-entity version and one literal-quote version). Recommend deletion of one copy and re-opening ISS-1747 OR filing a fresh issue. Three minors are presentation polish on EXP-407 footnote (i) — generalize beyond the two extreme readings, disambiguate "firmament Sun" phrasing, optionally show (R_S, R_M) calibration inline. No verdict change; structural kill is now overdetermined (linear-charity 97× inflation AND exact-geometry Moon-Sun co-location). queue_id 368 set on this review file, matches queue item.
